title: High Gas Hydrate and Free Gas Concentrations: An Explanation for Seeps Offshore South Mocha Island
abstract: High velocities (up to 1900 m/s) and low velocities (1260 m/s) associated with high gas hydrate (up to 20% of total volume) and free gas (up to 1.1% of total volume) concentrations were detected close to Mocha Island (~38°S) from seismic velocity analysis. A variable geothermal gradient (65–110 °C/km) was estimated. Gas hydrate dissociation processes are proposed as the main supply source for seeps near Mocha Island.

location.description: Offshore south Mocha Island (~38°S), central Chilean margin; cold seep and gas hydrate study

seismic_lines.name: RC2901-731 — ODP MCS line offshore Mocha Island (~38°S); R/V Conrad RC2901, 1988
SO161-35 — SPOC MCS line offshore Mocha Island (~38°S); R/V SONNE SO161, 2001

data.name: MCS Sections — Offshore South Mocha Island ~38°S
data.description: Two MCS seismic sections offshore south Mocha Island (~38°S) analyzed for gas hydrate (up to 20% total volume, velocity 1900 m/s) and free gas (1.1%, 1260 m/s); BSR-based geothermal gradient 65–110°C/km. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: Navigation:Primary
data.description: acquired during the Robert D. Conrad expedition RC2901 (1988)

_data_note: MDPI open-access paper. Uses MCS data from Chilean margin likely from SPOC/GEOMAR cruises. Open access PDF available.

processing.summary: Two marine seismic reflection sections offshore south Mocha Island (~38°S) were analyzed using velocity analysis and PSDM for BSR characterization and gas hydrate/free gas quantification. High hydrate concentrations (up to 20% total volume) and elevated geothermal gradients (65–110°C/km) are interpreted as the source of cold seep activity near Mocha Island.
workflow: 4. Gas hydrate and free gas concentration estimation using rock physics
5. Geothermal gradient calculation from BSR depth
processing.notes: RC2901-731 (R/V Conrad 1988): 3000m, 240ch, 12.5m intertrace, 50m shot spacing, 10 airguns/61.3L=3741cui. SO161-35 (R/V Sonne SPOC 2001): 3000m, 132ch (25+108 split at ch24: 12.5m/25m intertrace), 50m shot spacing, 20 airguns/54.1L=3302cui. Open-access MDPI paper.

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. CRITICAL: authors corrected from 6 to 4: Vargas-Cordero, Tinivella, Villar-Muñoz, Bento Joaquim P. (removed Fernandoy/Giustiniani/Loreto who are NOT in this paper). vessel: added R/V Conrad RC2901. year_acquired=1988. streamer=3000m,132ch(SO161)/240ch(RC2901). software=Seismic Unix+Kirchhoff PSDM. seismic_lines: RC2901-731 and SO161-35 named.
